reviews.title: It's a mouse, it works.
reviews.text:
When purchasing a bluetooth mouse, or any bluetooth device, an important factor is how well it connects and how well it STAYS connected. After all this time, the industry still can’t make up its mind about how bluetooth works:
- Microsoft with their mouse and Sony with their controller that pair without the need of a code (which Android’s bluetooth stack doesn’t support).
- Mice and keyboards that will not reconnect after it wakes from sleep mode.
- To this day, bluetooth devices still can’t wake a computer from suspend state.
So when I get a simple bluetooth mouse for a good price, and it actually works as expected, I was happy.

For my review, I’m comparing the Bornd mouse to the [[ASIN:B006428K7I Motorola 89519N Bluetooth Mouse]] which I got in a set that included the [[ASIN:B004L9MBH6 Motorola Wireless Keyboard for Motorola ATRIX and XOOM (Motorola Retail Packaging)]].

With the Motorola mouse, its frequency somehow conflicts with the Motorola keyboard, resulting in sticky keys, as in “I’m having a good daaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaay”. Conversely, the keyboard interferes with the mouse where the computer would actually lose connection with the mouse for a few seconds after typing, or lose connection completely.

What I liked about the Bornd:
- Pairing was simple using the button at the bottom of the mouse.
- Operating distance is about 5 feet, but this may be because of my computer’s bluetooth antenna
- Takes a single AA battery which lasts over a month of moderate use (2 hours per day)
- Mouse movement is good. No need to adjust mouse speed in the control panel.
- Accuracy is good. Editing images using Pixlr.com, I’m able to select precisely the parts of the image I need.

What gave me trouble with the Bornd:
- When you first put your hand on this mouse, you will not feel that it is very well built. The plastic is completely smooth and the mouse wheel seems to rattle when you shake it. I got used to it after a few minutes, though.
- The mouse reconnects with the computer most of the time, but sometimes it was necessary to turn the mouse off and on again after the computer reboots or if the computer was out of range for an extended period of time.
- Lately, I’ve been experiencing double-clicks when I click on the left mouse button once. I’m usually able to fix this by getting angry and slamming the mouse down a couple of times. This may indicate a build quality issue with this mouse.

In my specific case, the Bournd did not interfere with my Motorola keyboard at all and it seems the sticky key occurs only when the batteries on the keyboard runs low.

The device was recognized and connected to the notebook with no unusual delays or difficulties.  it works well with the PC as I recline in my easy-chair, using small motions upon the arm of the chair and allowing me to navigate through email and web browsing and to play games.  It is a nice compliment to the available on-screen gesture controls of the computer, especially for a die-hard convert from Windows XP and Windows 7 operating systems.

reviews.title: Good but wanted more features
reviews.text:
I bought this in a time crunch. My wired mouse was getting hard to use because the scroll wheel had shifted out of place. I had several wireless mice around, but none of them still had their wireless receiver. And they were before the days of Bluetooth. So, I had was in the market for a new one.

This one is nice. It fits the hand well and all. But, I realized that it did not have the features that I was used to. First, it is not a quiet mouse. I did not notice at first, but when it penetrated my fog of concentration, I could not unhear it. Click. Click. Click! And, this was not great for conference calls either.

The other issue was that it tracked slower than I would like. I was still using my wired mouse at this point, so I did not want to change mouse settings via software. I really missed having a dpi button on my mouse. This is important to me as I use a laptop on an extended display to a 2nd monitor. So, I need to be able to zip from one end of the screen the other - quick enough to unmute and answer a question during a conference call.

Have to click to wake up the mouse kept catching my attention - which means it was on it's way to annoying me... I have worked with computers for so long, it's like an extension of me. It needs to be intuitive to use and not interrupt my flow.

And, at this point, I figured out that I would need to use two different computers at once. And, while I could pair this wireless mouse with a wired one, the USB ports required were a challenge. My newer computer has no USB C ports at all. So, this limited my set-up options. Secondly, I now had 2 mice crowding limited desk space.

So, I learned that there was such a thing as multi-device mice. And, I found one that can switch between two computers and a iPad - with a simple LED indicator so that you know which device you are controlling. I bought the also designed to be silent and to switch between different dpi. All these features and it was only $3 more.

This is what I bought to replace this one - Arteck Multi-Device Wireless Bluetooth Mouse with Nano USB Receiver Ergonomic Right Hand Silent Clicking for Computer Desktop PC Laptop Mac iPad and Windows 10/8 iPad OS Build in Rechargeable Battery

So, to sum it up, I would say that I need the speed and responsiveness of a gamer's mouse with the silent clicks and 3 device connects like an office worker. So, this mouse is ok. But, if you are an advance or intense user, you may find that it does meet your needs.

reviews.title: Loud click, nice feel on your fingers, good for small hands
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Material on your hands feels silky. Great feel.
- Scroll wheel works nicely.
- Tracking is fine.
- Battery rather than recharge, which I like because all of the recharging ones die within a year
- On/Off switch to save your battery
- Excellent for smaller hands. I wear women's gloves size S, and this fits my hands really well.

Cons:
- The click. I have a thing with clicks. It's maddening to hear yourself click loudly all day. If the click were silent, this would be the perfect mouse. Maybe I'm a little neurotic, but it's distracting for me.

Neutral:
- The LED light near your thumb is small. I don't know why so many of mice have light strips. It seems like something else to use up the battery. I'd prefer to not have any lights, outside of what's necessary underneath for a track light. Not a big deal, and better than most.

Other:
- Made In China
- 6D Optical Mouse
- Takes 2 AAA Batteries

reviews.title: Lasted 5 months and dead
reviews.text: I bought this November  2022 and it stopped working mid-March 2023.  The design helped my carpal tunnel, and it was cheap enough, but I think the next one I get, I will spend a bit more and get better quality.

reviews.title: Sucks, Slow, Terrible, Missing pieces.
reviews.text:
Extremely slow unless you know how to disable 50 or so features that run off the bat. The mouse came with no bluetooth adapter, and this doesn't have built in bluetooth.
Seriously don't buy HP.

1 hour of ownership update: It's stopped opening windows. It brings up the login screen, then I get a black screen. The computer no longer works and I've done nothing to it. There are several other reviews referring to this issue.

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: so far its a great buy. I like it

reviews.title: Excelent value laptop
reviews.text: My daughter needed a faster more copact laptop for her graphics software so we gave this one a try.  Very fast, easy setup...nice and easy to chuck the bloatware.  A reasonable price made it an easy choice...so much so that we purchased another.

reviews.title: Definitely returning and buying a different brand
reviews.text: I had high hopes for this laptop... very very very slow. I mean how can a company like HP in this day in age make a laptop this slow. I didn’t expect speeds you would see in a gaming laptop for this price but I at least want to be able to watch a movie or even start the laptop within 10 minutes, and they don’t even allow you to upgrade the RAM or anything from what I can tell... and the battery life is laughable, like a previous comment said, about 1 percent per minute. I would even go as far as to say that it dies faster than that. Purchased this for my sister for her birthday but will definitely return and go with a different laptop.

reviews.title: Very Nice
reviews.text:
Very nice. Looks good, I like the gold edging more than I thought I would. A little small, but I barely notice. The mouse I WAS using was HUGE, and my entire palm rested on it. This one only covers ⅔ of my palm.

Very comfortable.

Have not played anything that could be considered a stress test. I like that you can turn it off, but I dislike that you have to remember to turn it off.

I cannot tell if it goes to sleep.

reviews.title: wireless mouse
reviews.text: My old wireless finally died - dropped too many times onto the hardwood floor.  I purchased one that was like the old one and this one.  This one is flatter.  I ended up choosing to use this one.  It loaded quickly and is very comfortable to use.  Very, very quiet click - almost silent.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

reviews.title: Good value for the price
reviews.text: This is a good value for the cost.  If you need something small and easy to carry around with you this is great. Especially right now I am between working from home but sometimes have to visit the office and the less and smaller items I carry, the better!  This is nice and slim and works great with my laptop.  I don't really like to use batteries but as long as you switch it off when not in use you will be able to get quite a bit of use out of one.

reviews.title: Loses Connection CONSTANTLY
reviews.text: I have to turn it off and on about 5x a day because it randomly loses connectivity. When I am using it or idle for less than 1 min.

reviews.title: Works well, light, easy.
reviews.text: This works really well, I do wish it had a little bit more weight to it.  I use the surface mouse generally and love it.  I bought this as a replacement bc I transitioned my surface mouse to my work computer. This works similarly well, it’s just so light it doesn’t feel as sturdy.  It won’t work with 2 devices at once, you have to keep switching, but does have multiple ways to connect.

reviews.title: Sharp, poor cursor control, poor scrolling
reviews.text: I had hoped, for this price, I might get something that worked...NOT!  The edges are sharp and very uncomfortable.  If you have tiny hands, this might work.  Scrolling is pretty much a bumpy ride, all or nothing, no matter how you set the scroll.  The cursor was very hard to control and very hard to get it in the right place.  Yes, I played with mouse settings and tried to tweak but no go.

reviews.title: Bad product

reviews.title: Lightweight and smooth.
reviews.text: Clicks easily and slides on mouse pad smoothly. A bit frustrating that it goes into sleep mode after a short time when I’m busy elsewhere but ease of use makes it okay. You need to be careful putting in the charger since the mouse is lightweight. And I love the pink color!

reviews.title: I like it
reviews.text: I like most of things to be wireless, and I really like this mouse because I have yet to have an issue with the bluetooth connection. I will say that you do have to push a bit harder to click the mouse than I am normally used to, but it's negligible and ultimately didn't bother me. I like the lights on it, and the side of the mouse works great for me (although it's a bit small for someone with big hands). I would buy again.

reviews.title: Goes to sleep when you need it the most
reviews.text: Won't stay connected to my laptop and goes to sleep.  Have to power cycle to get it to work again.  Even tried leaving the USB cable plugged in and it still goes to sleep.

reviews.title: Looks expensive but feels cheap
reviews.text:
The included USB charging line does not fit snug. Feels like an aftermarket adapter and felt as if the mouse would break is pushed all the way in or pulled out. Time will tell if the charging line lasts. I'll update as time goes on.

It has the feel of a toy but the girls in the house are already asking for one like it, so pretty sells. Color is towards the green scale but works well with seafoam colors.

title: Bluetooth Mouse for iPad MacBook Air Pro Mini Mac iPhone Tablet Cell Phone, Computer Wireless Mouse for Laptop PC Desktop, Dual Mode (Bluetooth + 2.4G), Portable Optical Cordless Mice Silent (Silver)
description:
Specifications:
Bluetooth Edition: Bluetooth 5.0 Mouse Dimensions: 4.21 X 2.36 X 1.26 inchs Mouse Weight: 2.05 ounces Operating Range: Up to 33 ft/ 10 meters Buttons Number: 3 Keys DPI Level: 1600 DPI Battery Powered: 1 AA battery powered,
Battery NOT Included
The mouse supports Bluetooth and 2.4GHz USB connection
Note: The Bluetooth Mouse only compatible with iPad OS/iOS 13 and above. So you need to update the system of your ipad and iphone to iPad OS/iOS 13 and above, and turn on “Assistive Touch”: Setting> Accessibility> Touch> Assistive Touch, then connect the mouse.
How to connect Bluetooth mouse:
1: Turn on the power ON/OFF, switch to Bluetooth. 2: Then Turn on the Bluetooth switch of your tablet or mobile phone, and search for Bluetooth mouse. 3: Click the Bluetooth mouse on tablet phone or other devices to connect to your device.
How to connect 2.4GHz USB mouse:
1: Turn on the power ON/OFF, switch to 2.4G. 2: Then insert the USB receiver into the USB port of the notebook or computer, and then you can use it.
Package Included:
1x Dual Mode Mouse (Bluetooth+2.4GHz) 1x USB Receiver 1x User Manual
Broad Compatibility:
Compatible with iPadOS iPad 10.2/9.7 5th 6th 7th 8th 9th gen, iPad Air 10.5/10.9, iPad Pro 11/12.9 2nd 3rd 4th 5th generation, iPad Mini 5/4/3, iPhone 12 11 pro max, iPhone X XS XR max, etc.
Q&A
Where is the USB receiver?
The USB receiver is in the mouse, you can find it when you open the mouse's battery cover. When you don't use it, you can plug the nano receiver into the USB storage slot again, so you don't lose the USB.
Connectivity and USB troubleshooting
Restart your computer and make sure the mouse is turned on and not too far away from the USB Receiver.
Warranty: 18 months warranty, and our friendly customer service.
Bluetooth mouse only compatible with iPad OS/ iOS 13 and above.
features: Wide Compatibility: The mouse compatible with Windows XP/ Vista/ 7/ 8/ 10/ 11, Chrome OS, Mac OS, Android, iOS, and perfect for laptop notebook PC computer desktop chromebook, Apple iPhone iPad, iPad Air, iPad Mini, iPad Pro, MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, Mac iMac Samsung Galaxy Tab/ Note/ S tablet smartphone Surface Pro and other multi device.【Bluetooth mouse compatible with iPad OS/ iOS 13 and above, turn on “Assistive Touch”: Setting> Accessibility> Touch> Assistive Touch】

Dual-Mode Mouse (2.4G + Bluetooth 5.0): 2.4GHz wireless mode, plug and play, no need to download drivers. Bluetooth mode, easy to connect and pair. Both modes provide a reliable and stable connection up to 33 feet with virtually no drop-outs and delays. The wireless mouse realizes free switches between two devices with one mouse only by one click. Note: You can't use Bluetooth and 2.4G mode at the same time. The USB dongle is stored in the compartment next to the mouse battery slot.

Comfortable & Quiet Click: The wireless mouse features a hand-friendly contoured shape for all-day comfort. Quiet clicks allow you to focus on your work without worrying about disturbing others or causing unwanted distracting noises. 1600 DPI cursor sensitivity provides smooth, precise tracking and scrolling for easy navigation on most surfaces. The sturdy rubber scroll wheel prevents your hands from slipping when rolling. The nano receiver is stored on the back of the mouse.

Long Battery Life: The mouse is powered by 1 AA battery (battery is not included). The mouse will enter into auto-sleep mode after 10 minutes of inactivity, and you can click any key to wake it up. Long battery life and working time, it can be used for months without battery replacement. There is an ON/ OFF button on the back of the mouse, which can be turned off to save power. Battery NOT included.

Protable & Durable Design: The ergonomic design and smooth surface fit your hands perfectly and provide you with a comfortable experience. You can take it anywhere you need it for office, home, meetings or travel. Tested over 3 million clicks to ensure mouse durability. What You Will Get: Wireless Bluetooth Mouse, USB Receiver, User Manual, 18 months warranty, and our friendly customer service.

reviews.title: I couldn't have asked for a better productivity tool
reviews.text:
This item has been with me for such a short time that I am still breaking it in. So far these are my notes.

This mouse takes advantage of both Bluetooth and 2.4G technology

The connection tends to become unstable after a few months, reconnect to solve the issue

If you are unfortunate enough to have more than one device with need for a mouse, switching between them will be time consuming and confusing due to the syncing required.

Its slim profile also makes it easy to store when not in use.

The battery life lasts for weeks

The power conserving switch saves battery life

Totally worth the price!

I hope this is helpful.

He says it's quiet and responsive. We haven't had it long enough to comment on battery longevity, but so far so good.

Automatically enter sleep mode after 5 minutes of inactivity to save battery, press any key to wake up immediately. The keyboard and mouse each use one AA battery (not included).
ꘖ⦺【Wide Compatibility】Wireless keyboard and mouse set compatible with Windows 98/Me/2000/XP/Vista/7/8/10/11 or latest. Work with Computer/Laptop/PC/Desktop.
❤【1 Year Warranty】Any questions will reply to you within 12 hours, please contact us via Amazon email in the first time and we will provide you with a satisfactory solution.

reviews.title: Excellent Keyboard!
reviews.text: This was a Christmas gift for my daughter and she absolutely loves everything about this keyboard. Thank you

reviews.title: Keyboard is very loud when typing!
reviews.text: The mouse is pretty standard, nothing great, but nothing bad. I brought my cute little set to work in my laptop bag so I figured it was secure. When I took the keyboard out 1 letter had popped off. No biggie, it snapped back on easily. The keyboard buttons are bit higher than I'm used to so I was typing slower and it was a bit uncomfortable after a while. I planned on switching back to my regular keyboard shortly but what escalated that switch was how loud the keyboard was while I was typing. My office is usually rather quiet plus I'm in a cubicle and a coworker asked what are you doing over there!?! He said it sounded like I was hammering the letters with a small hammer. I knew it was loud but after realizing it was bothering my coworkers I immediately put it in my bag to bring home. Haven't used it since!

reviews.title: Nice keyboard and mouse set
reviews.text: This is a great mouse and keyboard set, feels like typing on an old typewriter but more comfortable. I like the combined usb. Looks very nice, comfortable to use,

reviews.title: Amusing
reviews.text: I really like this set. They both match perfectly. The keyboard is comfortable and all the buttons work perfectly. I like the round buttons they are pretty cool. Connects easy enough. Just wish it was rechargeable.

reviews.title: Plug and Play Wireless Keyboard; Not sure about the mouse
reviews.text: I run Windows 10 on my laptop, and this wireless keyboard was up and running in few minutes after unpackaging.  You will need to provide your own batteries (2 AA – one for the mouse and one for the keyboard).  The keys are not tactile like other “typewriter” keyboard I have, but the keys have enough tension in the loaded spring to give a slight “satisfactory” feel to the keystrokes.  The wireless connection between the receiver and the keyboard/mouse was responsive and experienced no noticeable delay.  I am not 100% sold on the “milk tea” colored keyboard, but it slowly grew on me after a few days of use, although my 8-year young daughter thought it was like a puke color… an interesting observation.  The mouse was not for me.  It was too flat and too small for my hand, and after a few hours of cumbersome use, I switched back to my existing mouse pad.  For less than $49 USD, I think this keyboard brings a fun look to the otherwise a boring office environment.
